- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:

commit.message:
Fix markdown rendering: GitHub-style alerts and list styling (#1057) * Fix markdown rendering: GitHub-style alerts and list styling GitHub-style alert callouts (> [!NOTE], > [!TIP], > [!IMPORTANT], > [!WARNING], > [!CAUTION]) were displaying the raw [!TYPE] text inside plain blockquotes instead of rendering as styled callout boxes. Root cause: Python's markdown library merges adjacent blockquotes into one element even when separated by a blank line, and provides no built-in support for GitHub Flavored Markdown alert syntax. Fix: added preprocess_github_alerts() to app/routes/docs.py. This runs on the raw markdown source before the main markdown.Markdown renderer, converting each alert blockquote independently. The alert body is rendered through a dedicated lightweight markdown pass so inline and block markup (bold, code, links, lists) inside alert bodies is fully processed. The surrounding
elements are emitted as raw HTML blocks that the main renderer passes through unchanged. Five alert types are now styled with on-brand colours and Material Symbols icons (teal for Tip, blue for Note, purple for Important, amber for Warning, red for Caution). CSS was added to templates/docs/view.html. Also fixed unordered list rendering: added explicit list-style-type: disc/ decimal, display: list-item, and compact

-inside-

  • margin rules to prevent Python markdown's loose-list

    wrapping from making list items visually indistinguishable from regular paragraphs. https://claude.ai/code/session_015VgujMrehBJbCBfSbsU2yT * Fix GitHub-style alert preprocessor: module-level constants, robust blockquote continuation, unit tests (#1058) * Initial plan * Fix alert preprocessor: module-level constants, robust body collection, add unit tests Co-authored-by: timwonderer <76604824+timwonderer@users.noreply.github.com> --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: timwonderer <76604824+timwonderer@users.noreply.github.com> --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auto-shelve): Fix SSH error-handling pattern broken under bash -e (#162)

* Initial plan

* fix(automatic-instance-shelving): Handle missing GitHub issue gracefully

When gh issue view fails (e.g., instance has no corresponding GitHub
issue), emit a warning and skip the instance instead of crashing the
entire workflow. This prevents a single orphaned instance from blocking
auto-shelve for all subsequent instances.

Fixes #155

Co-authored-by: muratmaga <21285140+muratmaga@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Fix broken var=$(ssh ...) patterns under bash -e

Under GitHub Actions' default shell (bash -e), the pattern:
  var=$(cmd)
  if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then continue; fi

is broken: when cmd fails, set -e exits the script immediately
before the if-check runs. This caused the entire while loop to
crash whenever any SSH command failed (e.g., connection refused
-> exit 255), preventing all subsequent instances from being
checked and auto-shelved.

Fix all three SSH patterns in automatic-instance-shelving.yml and
both SSH patterns in collect-instance-uptime.yml by moving the
command into an `if !` condition, which suppresses set -e.

Co-authored-by: muratmaga <21285140+muratmaga@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace O(4^n) diffusion matrix with matrix-free O(2^n) vector op

Root cause of 3-codon (18-qubit) execution failure:
  D = np.outer(sv0, sv0.conj())  -- builds 262144x262144 complex128 matrix
  = ~1 TB RAM for n=18 --> OOM / server crash

Fix: D@x = 2*(psi0^H . x)*psi0 - x  is mathematically identical but
uses O(2^n) memory instead of O(4^n). Applied in both:
  - run_bio_grover_local       (the main simulation path)
  - build_bio_step_circuits    (the step-by-step navigator path)

Also fixed _probs() in build_bio_step_circuits to filter p < 1e-12
entries rather than returning all 262144 states in the JSON response.

Verified: n=6 max_err=6e-16, n=12 max_err=5e-15 (machine epsilon).
n=18 end-to-end: FOUND in 453ms, confidence=0.992.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix rendering pipeline: PtrSpanCopy direction, save_area1 coordinate remap, fullscreen stack overflow

Three critical fixes that together restore in-game terrain rendering:

1. PtrSpanCopy direction (Drawarea.cpp): ASM at 0x00445250 confirms
   this=SOURCE, param=DESTINATION (REP MOVSB: ESI→EDI). Our code had
   it reversed — copying blank dest into populated source.

2. draw_view coordinate remap (view.cpp): When drawing to save_area1,
   decomp remaps render_rect to (0,0) origin and adjusts start_scr_col/row
   and map_scr_x/y_offset. After drawing, restores original coordinates.
   Added PtrCopy from save_area1→render_area after draw_view returns.

3. TScreenPanel::set_focus stack overflow (Pnl_scr.cpp): Ghidra shows
   color_table[256] but ASM shows SUB ESP,0x408 = 1032 bytes = room for
   257 entries. GetPalette/GetPaletteEntries write 256 entries at
   color_table+1 (indices 1-256), so [256] overflows by 4 bytes.
   Fix: color_table[257].

Also: run_built_game.bat now passes FULLSCREEN for cnc-ddraw 8-bit
surfaces (Pitch=Width=640), and autoplay takes in-game screenshot.

AGENTS.md updated with array off-by-one and copy direction traps.

commit.message:
Fix MSVC CI failures: add CbcInstanceFeatures.cpp and stub setNode

Two issues caused Windows MSVS build failures:

1. CbcInstanceFeatures.cpp (added in 9a29a215) was missing from all
   libCbcSolver MSVC projects (v10/v14/v16/v17), causing LNK2019
   unresolved external symbol errors for CbcInstanceFeatures ctor,
   dtor, extract() and writeCsv().

2. The Debug|x64 and Release|x64 configs in libCbc.vcxproj do not
   define CBC_THREAD, so CbcThread.hpp uses its stub class (the #else
   branch).  The stub lacked setNode(), which was newly called in
   CbcModel.cpp (1afb06a2), causing C2039 compile error.  Added a
   no-op setNode(CbcNode*) to the stub class.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: randomise default marker window on load

Instead of always starting at offset 0 (the centre node around
c.5266dupC), pick a random offset in [-5, +5] on each page load and
on each codon-count change. This means every visit shows a different
nearby window of the BRCA1 hotspot.

Changes (GroverPOC.jsx only):
- markerOffset initial state uses a lazy initialiser: Math.floor(Math.random()*11)-5
- nCodons useEffect computes a fresh random offset, sets it, and passes
  it to fetchMarker() so both the API request and the display are in sync

fix: capture Win+Shift+S / Snipping Tool images via registered PNG format

Root cause: Win+Shift+S stores screenshots as the registered 'PNG' clipboard
format (RegisterClipboardFormatW). CF_DIB is only a synthesized alias —
GetClipboardData(CF_DIB) silently fails for this source, so both Qt's
clipboard.image() and PIL.ImageGrab.grabclipboard() return nothing.

Changes:
- Pre-register CF_PNG and CF_JFIF format IDs at module load time
- Include _CF_PNG / _CF_JFIF in has_image detection check
- Add _read_clipboard_format_bytes() helper: opens clipboard, calls
  GlobalLock/GlobalSize/GlobalUnlock to read raw bytes for any format
- Add Attempt 3 in _process_clipboard: read 'PNG'/'JFIF' bytes directly
  then decode with PIL and save — covers Win+Shift+S and Snipping Tool

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use generated image when thumbnail is a data URI

Crawlers (iMessage Applebot, Discordbot, etc.) cannot fetch data: URIs —
they make HTTP requests to external URLs. When Aws:BucketName is not
configured the storage service falls back to a base64 data URI, which
was placed verbatim in og:image and silently ignored by every crawler.

Changes:
- OgController: treat ThumbnailUrl values that don't start with 'http'
  as missing; fall back to the /og/events/{id}/image generated gradient.
- nginx.conf: add comment clarifying that iMessage uses Applebot UA so
  the existing UA map covers it without any extra entries.
- e2e/app.spec.ts: update thumbnail routing test to branch on whether
  the stored URL is a data URI or a real S3 URL, matching the new logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[minor][feature]: Add more execution flow tag point (#1694)

## PR Title Format

**Required Format:** `[Keyword1] [Keyword2]: Description`

- **Keyword1:** `major`, `minor`, or `patch` (case-insensitive)
- **Keyword2:** `feature`, `bugfix`, `engg`, or `tests`
(case-insensitive)

**Examples:**
- `[MAJOR] [Feature]: new API`
- `[minor] [bugfix]: fix crash`
- `[PATCH][tests]:add coverage`

## Proposed changes


This pull request introduces comprehensive execution flow logging across
multiple controller and request classes in the `IdentityCore` module.
The changes add logging tags at key decision points and completion
blocks, with contextual information such as error codes and correlation
IDs. This enhancement will improve traceability and debugging for silent
and interactive token requests, as well as SSO extension requests.

### Execution flow logging enhancements

* Added `MSIDExecutionFlowLogger` and `MSIDExecutionFlowConstants`
imports to `MSIDRequestControllerFactory.m`,
`MSIDSSORemoteInteractiveTokenRequest.m`, and
`MSIDSSORemoteSilentTokenRequest.m` to enable logging functionality.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-da918d3c617539935f2aa37a6c19ecc4ab145ef10acbe2f50a6e0220a0e43741R28-R29)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-3ff9a77cf45790833b234f0cda9bf177e4b2a774e0fd65018aae7a4223854da3R40-R41)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-a34cbe47f4e4bad7bc3877ddb4be242c48eecc433875802d4b916261ead46934R40-R41)
* Inserted execution flow logging tags at key points in silent and
interactive controller creation logic, including broker usage checks,
SSO extension checks, XPC mode checks, fallback handling, and controller
completion. Tags include relevant extra info and correlation IDs for
improved traceability.
